questions.specification: Logic gates and truth tables in digital systems.

parts.content: Explain how logic gates and truth tables are used in digital circuits.
parts.markscheme:
- **State** that logic gates are used to process binary signals ($0$ and $1$) according to Boolean logic rules. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Identify** fundamental gates, such as AND, OR, and NOT, as the building blocks of more complex digital systems. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Describe** how connecting multiple gates forms combinational logic circuits used for making decisions in hardware. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Explain** that truth tables are used to provide an exhaustive representation of all possible input combinations and their corresponding outputs. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Outline** the role of truth tables in the design, testing, and debugging phases to ensure circuit accuracy. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **List** examples of practical systems modeled using these components, such as alarm systems, calculators, and control units. <marks>1 mark</marks>

<note>
Award <marks>1 mark</marks> for each accurate explanation or example related to logic circuits up to <marks>6 marks</marks>.
</note>

questions.specification: A home appliance company is designing an automatic bathroom light system that turns on when someone enters, dims based on ambient light, and switches off after a set time delay using a basic logic circuit.

parts.content:
**Figure 1**: An image of a sensor commonly used in lighting systems.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/b8zdKPJuL1K_yy2d0KO1p)

From **Figure 1**, identify the type of sensor used and describe its purpose.
parts.markscheme:
- Sensor: Passive Infrared (PIR) sensor <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Purpose: Detects motion or body heat to trigger a response (e.g. turning on light) <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 2**: A basic setup showing an LDR (light-dependent resistor) in series with a fixed resistor connected to a microcontroller input pin.

*![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/TpGvWagdlHW33BHUOdsVb)*

Using **Figure 2**, explain how the LDR circuit works as a light sensor input.
parts.markscheme:
- As ambient light increases, LDR resistance decreases <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This alters voltage at the microcontroller input, signaling brightness change <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Referring to **Figure 1** and **2**, outline three advantages of combining a PIR sensor with an LDR in an automatic light system.
parts.markscheme:
- Ensures light only activates when motion is detected and room is dark <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Prevents unnecessary light activation during daytime <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Improves energy efficiency and user experience <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 3**: A truth table and logic diagram for a system using a NOT gate and AND gate to control a light output.



Using **Figure 3**, describe the logic behavior of the system and how the NOT gate functions.
![Image](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/bc1705dc-0e73-4262-9c13-e2cdd9242c7c)
parts.markscheme:
- NOT gate inverts the signal from LDR (dark becomes 1) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- AND gate receives two inputs: PIR and inverted LDR signal <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Output is high (light on) only when motion is detected and it's dark <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Light stays off if either input is 0 <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Suggest one way the system could be improved using a microcontroller instead of only logic gates.
parts.markscheme: - A microcontroller allows programmable time delay or sensitivity adjustments <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Design a brief sequence for how the microcontroller could operate this system, using pseudocode or steps.
parts.markscheme:
- Begin loop <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Read PIR input <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Read LDR input (via analog or threshold digital value) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- If motion detected AND room is dark, turn on light <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Wait for time delay (e.g. 30 seconds) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Turn off light, repeat loop <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Compare the use of sensors and output devices in the input–process–output (IPO) model.
parts.markscheme:
- **State** that sensors are used in the input stage to collect environmental data from the surroundings. <marks>1</marks>
- **Identify** types of sensors such as temperature, ultrasonic, or light sensors used for data capture. <marks>1</marks>
- **Describe** how output devices function in the final stage to communicate information or trigger physical changes. <marks>1</marks>
- **Identify** examples of output devices such as buzzers, motors, and displays. <marks>1</marks>
- **Explain** that input signals from sensors are processed by microcontrollers/CPUs to determine appropriate actions. <marks>1</marks>
- **Explain** that output devices rely on this processed data to complete programmed responses or actions. <marks>1</marks>

6 marks total

questions.specification: What distinguishes digital systems from analogue systems?
What do system diagrams represent?

options.content: The software flow of a program
The components and layout of an electronic system
The magnetic field of a circuit
The thermal efficiency of a power supply

questions.specification: Why is voltage important in electronic circuits?

options.content: It drives the flow of electric current
options.markscheme: Voltage, or potential difference, is the work done per unit charge required to move a charge between two points in a circuit, effectively providing the 'push' that drives the electric current.

questions.specification:
What component in this setup acts at the first stage of the input–process–output model?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXcYx7PTvRAuC_ivYmuw5pKwCVyfbycmPW0cwT9RWtck8F7W8AT609qDLkm_-rahr6v4l-fqRE3GKTtH1oFqxTf6j9lj8Ev0m4A3GVQxUWS9qz5oFMB8N4bXf50MJbacK1iuaS7g?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.markscheme: In the input–process–output (IPO) model, sensors provide the data or signals to the system, representing the first (input) stage.
A MOSFET (Metal-Oxide-Semiconductor Field-Effect Transistor) acts as a switch or amplifier within the processing or output interface stage.
A relay module is used to switch high-power devices and typically functions as part of the output interface or processing stage.
An output actuator (such as a fan or motor) represents the final stage of the IPO model.

questions.specification: A design team is developing a smart agricultural monitoring system to maintain crop health in remote greenhouses. The system uses sensors, control logic, and actuators to detect environmental changes and respond automatically.

parts.content:
**Figure 1**: Block diagram showing a temperature sensor connected to a microcontroller, which controls a ventilation fan.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/NxhlZztDTwgRu7uqUiAFf)

Identify two advantages of using a microcontroller in a greenhouse control system like the one shown in **Figure 1**.
parts.markscheme:
Can be easily reprogrammed to handle different conditions or thresholds. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Allows for automated, real-time control with multiple sensor inputs. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per valid advantage of microcontroller use.*

parts.content: Describe how open-loop control differs from closed-loop control in the context of **Figure 1**.
parts.markscheme:
Open-loop: System turns on the fan for a fixed duration regardless of temperature. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Closed-loop: System monitors temperature and adjusts fan speed accordingly. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Closed-loop systems include feedback to improve accuracy and responsiveness. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per relevant distinction between open- and closed-loop control.*

parts.content:
**Figure 2**: Graph comparing analog vs digital signal outputs from a moisture sensor over time.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/v1-HbEDhCaVvyvuohP7w6)

Referring to **Figure 2**, explain two key differences between analog and digital signals in control systems.
parts.markscheme:
Analog signals vary continuously over a range (e.g. 0–5V), allowing fine-grained sensor readings. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Digital signals are binary (e.g. 0 or 1), which are simpler for on/off decisions. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Analog signals require analog-to-digital conversion to be used by digital microcontrollers. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Digital signals are less susceptible to electrical noise and interference. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per clearly explained difference with reference to the graph.*

parts.content:
**Figure 3**: Photo of an Arduino with relay module used to control a 12V water pump from a soil moisture sensor.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/hzjyNSvB8DqI5DuqPdEvn)

State the role of the relay module in the system shown in **Figure 3**.
parts.markscheme:
The relay module acts as a switch to allow the low-voltage microcontroller to control a high-voltage device like a water pump. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for a correct statement of the relay module’s function.*

parts.content: Analyse how the system in **Figure 3** functions using the IPO (Input–Process–Output) model.
parts.markscheme:
Input: Soil moisture sensor detects water level in the soil. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Process: Microcontroller reads sensor data and compares it to a pre-set threshold. <marks>1 mark</marks>

If moisture is below threshold, it triggers the output. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Output: Microcontroller activates the relay. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Relay turns on the 12V water pump to irrigate the soil. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Once moisture level rises, pump is switched off automatically. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for each clearly explained step of the IPO model up to [6 max].*

parts.content: Justify the importance of feedback in a smart control system like the one used in the greenhouse scenario.
parts.markscheme:
Feedback allows the system to respond dynamically to real-time environmental changes. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Prevents overwatering or overheating by adjusting outputs continuously. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Improves system efficiency and crop health by maintaining optimal conditions. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Reduces the need for human intervention, making it suitable for remote locations. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per well-justified benefit of feedback in control systems.*

questions.specification: What type of circuit configuration allows current to split across multiple paths?
What does Ohm's law relate in a circuit?

options.markscheme: Ohm's law specifically relates potential difference, current, and resistance. Power is a separate concept defined as $P = VI$.
Ohm's law states that the potential difference across a conductor is proportional to the current through it ($V = IR$), thus relating potential difference, current, and resistance.
Capacitance is a property of capacitors relating charge and potential difference, not the relationship described by Ohm's law.
Frequency is a characteristic of alternating currents or waves and is not the relationship defined by Ohm's law.

questions.specification:
In this digital communication diagram, what do the arrows represent?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXdX_sFuuCcXpYlN-R0PJNYEHuUNSVagvx2HMJyj7xFHFIvky73IZ7aqyHBOIYQp9UtF2FkIaj1BLHWp8N9cSIrNNzwr9_e4T1d04lyCWQhOMoeFK12JQlkAFfgY5qdO_-b9unL2UQ?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)
What is the result of increasing the resistance $R$ in a circuit, assuming a constant potential difference $V$?

options.markscheme: According to Ohm's Law, $I = \frac{V}{R}$. If the potential difference $V$ remains constant and the resistance $R$ increases, the current $I$ must decrease.
The current $I$ is inversely proportional to the resistance $R$ when the potential difference $V$ is constant. Therefore, an increase in $R$ leads to a decrease, not an increase, in $I$.
The power dissipated in a circuit with constant potential difference is given by $P = \frac{V^2}{R}$. If $R$ increases while $V$ is constant, the power $P$ will decrease.
The question specifies that the potential difference $V$ remains constant.

labeledSubSubTopics.path: B. Design in practice > B3.4 Electronic systems application and selection (HL only) > B3.4.7 Input sensors in digital systems
labeledSubSubTopics.title: B3.4.7 Input sensors in digital systems
labeledSubSubTopics.reasoning: The question specifically asks for the function of a photoresistor (a sensor) within a digital system, which matches the title of this leaf topic exactly.

labeledSubSubTopics.path: A. Design in theory > A3.4 Introduction to electronic systems (HL only) > A3.4.7 Input devices and sensors
labeledSubSubTopics.title: A3.4.7 Input devices and sensors
labeledSubSubTopics.reasoning: A photoresistor is a specific type of input device/sensor, and this topic covers the general theory and characteristics of such components.

parts.content: Describe the function of a photoresistor in a digital system.
parts.markscheme:
- Identifies that the photoresistor senses/detects ambient light level (light intensity) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Describes that its resistance varies with light level (typically decreases as light increases), providing a variable electrical signal/input for the control system <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Explain how flow diagrams help in designing electronic systems.
parts.markscheme:
- **Identify** that flow diagrams provide a visual representation of the logic and sequence of system operations. <marks>A1</marks>

- **State** that flow diagrams help model programmable systems through the use of standardized logic symbols. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Explain** how flow diagrams allow designers to identify errors or inefficiencies before physical construction. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Outline** how they facilitate clear communication between different team members, such as hardware engineers and programmers. <marks>A1</marks>

4 marks total

<note>Award [1] for each clear, relevant explanation up to a maximum of [4].</note>

parts.content: Describe how voltage, current and resistance are related in Ohm’s Law.
parts.markscheme:
- **State** that the current ($I$) through a conductor is directly proportional to the potential difference ($V$) across it <marks>1 mark</marks>
- **Identify** that this relationship holds only if the temperature and other physical conditions of the conductor remain constant <marks>1 mark</marks>
- **Describe** the relationship between current ($I$) and resistance ($R$) as being inversely proportional for a fixed potential difference ($I \propto \frac{1}{R}$) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- **Express** the relationship between all three quantities mathematically using the formula $V = IR$ (where $R$ is the constant of proportionality) <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: What component is essential for controlling current in a circuit?
Which formula correctly calculates electrical power ($P$)?
What happens in the second stage of the input–process–output model?

options.content: Signals are transmitted via wireless systems

parts.content: State two differences between using resistors in series and in parallel.
parts.markscheme:
- State that for resistors in series, the total resistance $R$ increases as more resistors are added, whereas for resistors in parallel, the total resistance $R$ decreases as more resistors are added <marks>A1</marks>

- State that for resistors in series, the potential difference $V$ is shared among the resistors, whereas for resistors in parallel, the potential difference across each resistor is the same <marks>A1</marks>

2 marks total

<note>Award [1] for each correct difference. Accept other valid differences, such as:
- In series, the current $I$ is the same through all resistors; in parallel, the total current divides among branches.
- In series, if one resistor fails, the entire circuit is broken; in parallel, if one resistor fails, the other resistors continue to function.</note>

questions.specification: What is one advantage of using resistors in series?

options.markscheme: In a series circuit, the total potential difference is distributed across the resistors. This principle is used in potential divider circuits to obtain specific voltages from a source.

parts.content: Discuss the benefits of using microcontrollers in electronic product design.
parts.markscheme:
- Reduced circuit complexity: They allow complex tasks to be automated within simple circuits, which reduces the physical size and complexity of the product. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Versatility through programmability: Being programmable allows the same chip to be configured for specific tasks such as timing, sensing, or control, providing high flexibility. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Lower component count: They reduce the need for multiple discrete components (e.g., logic gates), simplifying the circuit board and reducing assembly costs. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Ease of modification: Product functionality can be updated or corrected through software changes rather than requiring expensive hardware redesigns. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Design efficiency and reuse: A single microcontroller model can be reused across different product designs, improving manufacturing efficiency and reducing inventory. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Real-time monitoring: They enable continuous monitoring and adjustment of inputs, allowing for more precise control and user feedback. <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: What is the purpose of a flow diagram in an electronic system?

options.content: To test the physical layout of a circuit
To map how the system performs a specific function

questions.specification:
What is the main function of the component circled in the circuit diagram below?

![Circuit diagram with an inductor circled](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/plots/3411bdd2-8437-4683-81fd-5b33667b7639.png)

options.content: Regulates the direction of current flow
options.markscheme: This describes the function of a diode, which allows current to flow in only one direction.
The circled component is an inductor ($L$). According to Lenz's law, an inductor induces a back electromotive force (EMF) that opposes any change in the current flowing through it.

options.content: Protects the circuit from excessive current
options.markscheme: This describes the function of a fuse or circuit breaker, which breaks the circuit when the current exceeds a certain threshold.

options.content: Converts alternating current to direct current
options.markscheme: This describes the function of a rectifier circuit (typically using diodes), which converts bidirectional AC to unidirectional DC.

parts.content: Analyse how embedded systems enable communication between digital devices.
parts.markscheme:
Indicative content:

- **Hardware-Software Interfacing**: Embedded systems utilize specialized network interface hardware (such as RF transceivers or Ethernet controllers) that is controlled via low-level firmware drivers to translate software commands into physical signals. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Protocol Stack Management**: They implement standardized communication protocols (e.g., TCP/IP, Bluetooth, or Zigbee) in firmware, providing a logical structure for data exchange and ensuring interoperability between heterogeneous devices. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Data Encapsulation and Framing**: The embedded processor handles packetization, where raw data is wrapped in protocol-specific headers containing addressing, sequencing, and metadata required for network routing. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Signal Modulation and Demodulation**: Embedded modems or Digital-to-Analog Converters (DAC) convert digital binary data into analog signal variations (such as phase, amplitude, or frequency) suitable for wireless or wired transmission. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Reliability and Error Control**: Embedded systems implement mathematical algorithms like Cyclic Redundancy Checks (CRC) to verify data integrity upon receipt, managing retransmission requests (ARQ) to maintain connection stability. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Real-time Interrupt Handling**: Embedded systems use priority interrupts to process incoming communication packets immediately, ensuring low-latency responses which are critical for real-time control applications. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Resource Management (Buffering)**: Onboard memory (RAM) is used for buffering data streams, allowing the system to manage asynchronous data flows and prevent data loss when transmission and processing speeds differ. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Security Mechanisms**: Many embedded systems feature hardware-based cryptographic engines that perform high-speed encryption (e.g., AES or TLS) to protect data privacy and integrity during transmission. <marks>A1</marks>

- **Network Integration and Automation**: By abstracting hardware details into application layers, embedded systems enable complex device ecosystems (IoT) where devices can trigger automated actions based on exchanged sensor data. <marks>A1</marks>

<note>
Award [1] for each point of analysis that explains the relationship between hardware, software, or data processing mechanisms in enabling communication, up to [9 marks].
</note>

9 marks total

questions.specification: What do digital systems use to compare input values?

In this system diagram, what does the central 'Process' block usually represent?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/plots/22725d93-8180-4018-9b43-ffb55d65d28c.png)

options.markscheme: In an Input-Process-Output (IPO) system diagram, the 'Process' block represents the control logic or the component that performs operations on the input signals. This is typically a microcontroller, microprocessor, or a set of logic gates. Options B, D, and E represent storage, output devices, and specific signal modification stages respectively.

questions.specification:
What key function does the logic gate shown in this image perform?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/plots/8a5b5f81-a977-4cea-b4f7-bf5cd536802e.png)

options.markscheme: Adding input voltages is a characteristic function of an analogue summing amplifier (using operational amplifiers), not a digital logic gate.

options.content: Outputs $1$ only if both inputs are $1$
options.markscheme: The image shows a standard 2-input AND gate. The defining characteristic of an AND gate is that its output is high ($1$) only when all of its inputs are high ($1$).
Filtering frequencies is a function of high-pass or low-pass RC/RL filter circuits, not discrete logic gates.
Clock speed regulation is typically performed by timing circuits like crystal oscillators or phase-locked loops (PLLs), not a single AND gate.

parts.content: Evaluate the importance of accurate circuit diagrams in developing functional electronic systems.
parts.markscheme:
- **Identify** that circuit diagrams allow for the visual communication of how components connect and interact within a system. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Explain** that the use of standardised symbols ensures clarity and consistency, allowing diagrams to be understood globally. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Describe** how diagrams facilitate the designing, testing, and troubleshooting phases, reducing time spent on physical trial and error. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Analyze** their role as a blueprint, enabling the accurate construction of physical circuits from a conceptual model. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Explain** how accurate diagrams reduce the risk of critical errors, such as short circuits, polarity issues, or incorrect voltage levels. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Evaluate** the importance of documentation for future use, ensuring the system can be upgraded, maintained, or repaired by others. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **State** how they facilitate communication between different stakeholders, such as engineers, designers, and technicians. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Outline** their role in software development, aiding programmers in defining control logic by identifying input/output pins and signal paths. <marks>1 mark</marks>

- **Discuss** their necessity for safety compliance and ensuring that prototypes meet functional and regulatory specifications. <marks>1 mark</marks>

<note>Award [1] per relevant evaluative point or reason with clear explanation up to [9 max].</note>

questions.specification: Why are capacitors used in timing circuits?

options.content: They delay the flow of current

questions.specification: Which component stores charge and can be used to smooth potential difference?

Why would an engineer choose to use a resistor in this circuit?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXfkcs9zPQ4NWweAUcEYhwnlu7Mj5buc239K-7Sw1wzqh6zr6oDUdXtfotEQml7TFqEifFky7O6Ux8Mrt4gdINup4EDnsE_Jj5zasXrJaNbUT3RpbvuqKnLKB7ReoPFKreeezEAI?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

options.content: To control and limit current flow
To convert AC to DC power

questions.specification:
Which quantity is calculated using Ohm’s Law for the resistor in this setup?

![](https://pub-images.ai-solutions.org/1c4d792f-b40b-426b-ae16-081839178877.png)

options.markscheme: Energy efficiency is typically calculated as the ratio of useful power output to total power input, $\eta = \frac{P_{\text{out}}}{P_{\text{in}}}$, and is not a direct application of Ohm's Law.

Inductive reactance ($X_L = 2\pi fL$) applies to inductors in AC circuits. While it follows a similar mathematical form to Ohm's Law ($V = IX_L$), Ohm's Law specifically describes the constant ratio of potential difference to current for an ohmic conductor.

Ohm's Law states that the potential difference ($V$) across an ohmic conductor is proportional to the current ($I$) flowing through it, provided temperature remains constant ($V = IR$). In this setup, $V$ can be calculated if $I$ and $R$ are known.

Heat dissipation (electrical power) is calculated using Joule's Law, $P = I^2R$ or $P = VI$, which involves energy transfer over time, rather than just the linear relationship between $V$ and $I$ defined by Ohm's Law.

questions.specification: A group of students is developing a smart automation project using simple electronic components and a microcontroller. They are experimenting with sensors and actuators to create systems that respond to environmental changes and user inputs.

parts.content:
**Figure 1** shows a light-dependent resistor (LDR) connected in a voltage divider circuit with a fixed resistor. The output voltage is connected to a microcontroller input, and an LED is connected to a digital output pin.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/xn4JjoUm-rSE4buj2Ah_Q)

State the type of signal (analogue or digital) produced by the LDR in **Figure 1**.
parts.markscheme:
Analogue <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for identifying signal type.*

parts.content: Explain how the microcontroller in **Figure 1** processes input from the LDR to turn on an LED when light levels fall.
parts.markscheme:
The voltage across the LDR changes depending on light intensity. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Microcontroller compares this voltage to a threshold and outputs HIGH when it’s below it. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for accurate sensing explanation and [1] for response logic.*

parts.content:
**Figure 2** shows a simple control system where a DC motor is connected to an Arduino microcontroller. A pushbutton on the breadboard allows user interaction to activate the motor. Supporting components such as resistors and a capacitor are used for signal stability and protection.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/TU81rW-Dx_vVOhlAJHPWK)

Describe how this setup functions as a basic control system using the IPO (Input–Process–Output) model.
parts.markscheme:
Input: Pushbutton is pressed by the user. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Process: Arduino detects button press via digital input. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Process: Microcontroller executes code to respond to input. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Output: Arduino sends HIGH signal to the motor control pin. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Output: DC motor is powered and begins to rotate. <marks>1 mark</marks>

System demonstrates a basic user-triggered control loop. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for each clearly explained IPO stage with relevance to the image. Max [6].*

parts.content:
**Figure 3** shows an ultrasonic sensor mounted to detect distance from objects, connected to a microcontroller. A buzzer is attached to give auditory feedback if objects come within a certain range.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/3WM5v7bG37SiwnmM7_RME)

Identify and explain the input, process, and output components in **Figure 3**.
parts.markscheme:
Input: Ultrasonic sensor detects distance to an object. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Process: Microcontroller compares distance to preset threshold. <marks>1 mark</marks>

If object is too close, microcontroller sends signal. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Output: Buzzer is activated to alert user. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] for each correct IPO stage.*

parts.content: Discuss how feedback and control improve system efficiency in any two of the circuits shown.
parts.markscheme:
In **Figure 1**, LDR provides continuous input; feedback allows dynamic light control. <marks>1 mark</marks>

System automatically adjusts LED based on environment, conserving energy. <marks>1 mark</marks>

In Figure 3, distance sensor monitors real-time position of objects. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Microcontroller dynamically adjusts output, activating buzzer only when needed. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Feedback prevents unnecessary activation and improves accuracy. <marks>1 mark</marks>

Both systems use feedback to operate autonomously and efficiently. <marks>1 mark</marks>

*Award [1] per valid point up to [6 max].*

questions.specification: A theme park is designing an automated control system for a small ride. The system must manage safety inputs, activate output devices, and operate based on predefined logic using a microcontroller and sensors.

parts.content:
**Figure 1**: A basic Arduino circuit that uses a push-button to control a DC motor.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/LtsX5Nb944ZCbnFxzwvA7)

State the role of the push-button in the control system shown in **Figure 1**.
parts.markscheme: - The push-button serves as a digital input to the microcontroller to trigger motor activation. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 2**: An Arduino system using a thermistor and LCD display with multiple push buttons.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/QiT3bzHEYpiZFZNLWhwc5)

Describe how the thermistor functions as an input device in **Figure 2**.
parts.markscheme:
- The thermistor changes resistance based on temperature. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This change alters the voltage sent to the microcontroller's analog pin. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Outline how feedback is used in the system shown in **Figure 2**.
parts.markscheme:
- The thermistor provides continuous temperature readings to the microcontroller (sensor feedback of the environment). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- The microcontroller repeatedly compares the measured value to a set point/threshold and updates the system state accordingly. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- The LCD output is updated based on the latest sensor reading (e.g., current temperature and/or a warning message), providing feedback to the user. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Using the IPO (Input–Process–Output) model, analyse how the system in **Figure 2** responds to rising temperature.
parts.markscheme:
- Input: Thermistor detects rising temperature (resistance changes, producing a changing analog voltage). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Process: Microcontroller reads the analog value and converts it to a temperature value. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Process: Microcontroller compares the measured temperature to a pre-set threshold. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- If temperature exceeds the threshold, a control decision is made to change the output state. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Output: LCD displays the current temperature and/or a warning/alert message. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Continuous sensing and updating enables monitoring of environmental conditions for safety. <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content:
**Figure 3**: A truth table showing logic states (A and B) and corresponding LED outputs for a traffic light system.

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/question-images/BQZmtgYybl6qWnv8BSFak)

Explain how the sequence of outputs in **Figure 3** could control a set of traffic lights.
parts.markscheme:
- Each row in the truth table represents a different step/state in the sequence. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- The microcontroller uses inputs $A$ and $B$ to select which output(s) are ON. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Some states can deliberately have two lights ON at once (e.g., Red and Yellow together) to indicate a transition between phases. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- This produces a safe and consistent traffic light cycle (e.g., Red $ Red+Yellow $ Green $ Yellow). <marks>1 mark</marks>

parts.content: Justify why using a truth table like **Figure 3** is useful for programming microcontroller-based control systems.
parts.markscheme:
- Truth tables simplify logic by listing all possible input combinations and corresponding outputs. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- They help map input states directly to output actions, which can be implemented as conditional logic in code. <marks>1 mark</marks>
- They support systematic checking/testing of all states (including transition states such as Red+Yellow). <marks>1 mark</marks>
- They improve accuracy and reduce logic errors in embedded code. <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification: In a digital system, what happens at the first stage of the input–process–output model?

options.content: Inputs are used to sense changes in the environment
Signals are converted to digital output
Power is supplied to the system

questions.specification: What is the final stage of the input–process–output model responsible for?

options.content: Controlling or communicating with the environment

questions.specification: What is the role of resistors and capacitors in a circuit?

options.content: To control current and store electrical charge

questions.specification:
What does this type of diagram most likely represent in an electronic circuit?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/plots/58a3fee4-0ff8-4709-92c8-0224046ad7b8.png)

options.markscheme: Incorrect. A digital feedback control loop would typically include components such as a comparator, a digital controller, and a feedback path from an output sensor, which are not shown in the diagram.
Incorrect. A mixed analogue-digital processor diagram would show blocks for analogue-to-digital conversion (ADC) and digital-to-analogue conversion (DAC) alongside processing units.

Correct. The diagram illustrates a single input current $I$ branching into multiple parallel paths ($I_1, I_2, I_3$), which is a standard representation of current distribution at a junction in accordance with Kirchhoff's First Law.
Incorrect. A thermal regulation system diagram would focus on temperature sensors, heating or cooling elements, and a control mechanism to maintain a specific temperature.

questions.specification: What does an embedded system do in digital electronics?

labeledSubSubTopics.path: A. Design in theory > A3.4 Introduction to electronic systems (HL only) > A3.4.12 Embedded systems communication
labeledSubSubTopics.title: A3.4.12 Embedded systems communication
labeledSubSubTopics.reasoning: The question asks about the function of an embedded system in digital electronics, specifically focusing on its role in allowing systems to communicate, which directly aligns with topic A3.4.12 'Embedded systems communication'.

options.markscheme: This describes energy storage (e.g., batteries/capacitors), not an embedded system.
This describes CAD/EDA software tools, not an embedded system.

options.content: Controls a specific function within a larger product or system
options.markscheme: An embedded system is a microcontroller/computer built into a device to perform a dedicated control task.
This describes shielding/filtering/EMC techniques rather than the purpose of an embedded system.

questions.specification: What does a digital system at the third stage of the input–process–output model do?

options.markscheme: This describes the input stage where sensors detect changes in the physical environment.
This describes the processing stage where the CPU or microcontroller handles data and instructions.

options.content: Use output to control external environment
options.markscheme: The third stage is Output. In a digital control system, this involves using actuators to affect or control the external environment based on processed data.
Measuring a physical property like inductance is a specific type of input sensing.

questions.specification: How are components typically arranged in a system diagram?

parts.content: Outline two output device examples and their role in a digital system.
parts.markscheme:
- Identify a valid output device example (e.g., LCD display/monitor) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Outline its role in the system (e.g., provides visual information/feedback to the user) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Identify a second valid output device example (e.g., motor/actuator/printer) <marks>1 mark</marks>
- Outline its role in the system (e.g., converts electrical signals into physical movement or produces a hard copy) <marks>1 mark</marks>

questions.specification:
What does this apparatus most likely assist with in electronic product manufacturing?

![](https://lh7-rt.googleusercontent.com/docsz/AD_4nXeOX3QbtbqUyNm-3gcm71QPRusGZqQQJPvgZHYMSFiTz5fpzpScKLXPNNu52LyGYjisXXfjlJEjKpa1Bj4Hh9zwZ_THKzVB5fKShe2Pf1rb9AhCzNgJ5x7f0S8ahpO8GuIQaND6qw?key=gz2IFjHMQBPKFRswYwU8xQ)

What component is most essential for executing a programmed control task in this circuit?

![](https://pub-images.revisiondojo.com/notes/b571194a-5719-48ac-b587-7cb8684c6164.jpeg)

options.markscheme: A resistor is a passive component used to limit current flow to protect other components, but it cannot execute programmed logic.
A breadboard provides a platform for prototyping and connecting components physically, but it has no processing or control capabilities.

options.content: LED used as a status indicator
options.markscheme: An LED (Light Emitting Diode) is an output device that provides visual feedback; it does not process instructions or control the task.
The microcontroller is the 'brain' of the circuit; it contains the processor and memory needed to store and execute the programmed instructions that define the control task.

topic.title: B3.4 Electronic systems application and selection (HL only)
